Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants a DuckDB SQL query that 1 Gets the ordered sequence of activities per case 2 Groups cases by sequence to find variants and counts 3 Finds top K variants by frequency 4 Returns all events from event_log that belong to cases in the top K variants

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In DuckDB we can use list or string agg to create the sequence list activity ORDER BY timestamp is very natural in DuckDB

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Steps 1 CTE case_sequences Group by case_id create variant as list activity ORDER BY timestamp 2 CTE variant_counts Group by variant count cases 3 CTE top_k_variants Use ORDER BY case_count DESC LIMIT K or QUALIFY with row_number Since K is a parameter we can use a placeholder like k or just LIMIT K 4 Join event_log with case_sequences and filter where variant is in top_k_variants

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets double check if list is the right function Yes DuckDB has list aggregate function Alternatively string_agg activity ORDER BY timestamp could be used which might be easier to read for some and standard SQL list is fine

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait using list in IN might be okay since DuckDB supports list comparisons but sometimes string_agg is safer for IN clauses Lets use string_agg activity ORDER BY timestamp for better readability and safer IN matching

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This perfectly matches all requirements 1 Construct sequence per case_id ordered by timestamp 2 Group by sequence count cases 3 Top K variants by frequency using LIMIT K 4 Return all events from filtered cases
